name: AdvancedFilterBuilder
description: A sophisticated filter builder component for creating complex query conditions with field selection, operators, values, and nested logical grouping (AND/OR/NOT). Supports recursive rule groups, multiple field types (text, number, select, date, boolean), and query string generation/parsing.
usage_guidelines: Use for advanced search and filtering interfaces where users need complex query capabilities.
Define available fields with types and operators using the FieldConfig array.
Support nested rule groups with AND/OR/NOT logical operators for complex conditions.
Handle filter changes via onChange callback which provides both tree structure and query string.
Use onApply callback to execute the filter when user clicks Apply button.
Use onReset callback to handle filter reset actions.
Provide field-specific input components based on field type (text, number, select, date, boolean).
Export/import filter configurations using toQueryString and parseQueryString utilities.

fields.description: Array of available fields for filtering. Each field has key, label, type, and optional options for select fields. Defaults to predefined fields including status, price, category, created_at, name, is_featured, and rating.

value.description: Controlled filter tree configuration. FilterTree is a RuleGroup containing nested rules and groups with logical operators.

onChange.description: Callback fired when filter configuration changes. Receives both the tree structure and generated query string.

onApply.description: Callback fired when user clicks Apply Filter button. Receives both the tree structure and generated query string.

onReset.description: Callback fired when user clicks Reset button. Receives the reset tree structure and empty query string.

properties.label: string - Display label for the field
properties.options: Array<{value: string, label: string}> - Options for select type fields

properties.operator: string - Comparison operator (is, is_not, contains, greater_than, etc.)
properties.logicalOperator: 'AND' | 'OR' | 'NOT' - Logical operator connecting to previous rule

RuleGroup.description: Group of rules with logical condition

properties.children: Array<Rule | RuleGroup> - Nested rules and groups
properties.logicalOperator: 'AND' | 'OR' | 'NOT' - Logical operator connecting to previous group

FilterTree.description: Root filter tree structure (alias for RuleGroup)

toQueryString.description: Converts FilterTree to query string format

parseQueryString.description: Parses query string back to FilterTree structure
parseQueryString.example: Converts 'status=active AND (price>100 OR category=books)' to FilterTree

createEmptyRule.description: Creates a new empty rule with default values

createEmptyGroup.description: Creates a new empty rule group

styling_info.visual_description: Hierarchical structure with nested rule groups. Each rule displays field selector, operator selector, value input, and delete button. Groups show with border and padding, containing multiple rules/groups. 'Where' label for first rule, logical operator selectors (AND/OR) for subsequent rules. Add Rule and Add Group buttons at bottom of each group. Apply Filter and Reset buttons in footer.

RuleComponent.description: Individual rule row with field, operator, value selectors and delete button

InputField.description: Dynamic input field that renders appropriate input based on field type (text input, numeric input, select, date picker, boolean select)

Do: Define clear field types and appropriate operators for each type
Provide meaningful field labels for better user experience
Handle onChange to track filter state in parent component
Use onApply to execute actual filtering logic
Provide options array for select-type fields
Use toQueryString utility to generate API-compatible query strings
Use parseQueryString to restore saved filter configurations
Handle empty or invalid filter states gracefully
Provide clear visual hierarchy for nested groups
Dont: Don't allow infinite nesting (component handles this internally)
Don't forget to handle empty rules when applying filters
Don't use for simple filtering (use basic filter components instead)
Don't forget to validate field values before submission
Don't modify the FilterTree structure directly (use provided callbacks)
Don't forget to provide options for select-type fields
Don't use complex field keys with special characters in query strings

examples.title: Basic Usage
With Custom Fields
Parsing Saved Filters

query_string_format.description: Generated query strings use special operators for different comparison types
examples: status=active AND price>100 - status is active AND price greater than 100
(status=active OR status=pending) AND price>100 - nested group with OR condition
